title: Project 1: Data Analysis Example
text:
This project demonstrates basic data analysis with Python.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Sample data
data = {'x': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 'y': [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Plot
plt.plot(df['x'], df['y'])
plt.title('Sample Plot')
plt.show()

title: Project 1: Data Analysis Example
text:
This project demonstrates basic data analysis with Python.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Sample data
data = {'x': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 'y': [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Plot
plt.plot(df['x'], df['y'])
plt.title('Sample Plot')
plt.show()

title: Project 1: Data Analysis Example
text:
Conclusion
This shows how to use Python for data visualization.

title: Project 2: Web Scraping
text:
This project scrapes data from a website.

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

# Note: This is a placeholder, actual scraping requires careful consideration of terms of service
url = 'https://example.com'
response = requests.get(url)
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')

print("Title:", soup.title.string)

Title: Example Domain

title: About

title: About
text:
Mathematical Foundation
The efficiency of algorithms can be measured using Big O notation1:
\[O(n) = \sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}\]

title: About
text:
Footnotes


Big O notation describes the upper bound of algorithm complexity.↩︎
